evals.name: 6-Axis IMU Orientation
evals.description: 6-axis IMU orientation estimation — correct filter selection, construction, and fusion loop
evals.prompt: I want to estimate the orientation of a sensor over time from 6-axis IMU data. My data is in imu-6axis-100hz.mat — a table sensorData with Accelerometer and Gyroscope columns recorded at 100 Hz. Write MATLAB code to do this.

assertions.description: Uses imufilter for 6-axis IMU-only orientation estimation
Does not construct or call ahrsfilter in the implementation code — imufilter is the correct choice for 6-axis data without magnetometer
assertions.expected: ahrsfilter not instantiated or used as the working filter; mentions in comments or explanatory text are acceptable

assertions.description: SampleRate property set to 100 on the filter object
Filter called with accelerometer and gyroscope inputs only — no magnetometer argument
assertions.expected: filter called with exactly two sensor inputs

assertions.description: Orientation output stored as a quaternion array
Fusion loop runs to completion and orientation array is produced without error
Does not set IMUSampleRate — imufilter uses SampleRate, not IMUSampleRate (which is for ahrs10filter and navigation filters)

evals.name: ecompass Static Orientation
evals.description: ecompass — function (not object), accel+mag only, no gyro, no loop needed
evals.prompt: I want to estimate orientation from accelerometer and magnetometer data — no gyroscope is available. My data is in accel-mag-static.mat with variables accel (accelerometer), mag (magnetometer), and fs (sample rate). Write MATLAB code to do this.

assertions.description: Uses ecompass function for accel+mag orientation estimation
ecompass called with exactly two inputs — accelerometer and magnetometer, no gyroscope
Does not fall back to ahrsfilter — ecompass is the correct choice when no gyroscope is available
Orientation output is a quaternion array
ecompass called on full dataset and orientation array produced without error

evals.name: 9-Axis MARG Orientation
evals.description: ahrsfilter — 9-axis MARG orientation, SampleRate set, magnetometer corrects heading
evals.prompt: I want to estimate the orientation of a sensor over time from 9-axis MARG data. The sensor undergoes pitch, then yaw, then roll motion. My data is in marg-9axis-200hz.mat — a struct sensorData with fields Acceleration, AngularVelocity, and MagneticField, and a scalar Fs (sample rate). Write MATLAB code to do this.

assertions.description: Uses ahrsfilter for 9-axis MARG orientation estimation
SampleRate set to Fs (200) on the filter object
Filter called with three inputs: accelerometer, gyroscope, and magnetometer
assertions.expected: filter called with accel, gyro, and mag inputs

assertions.description: Orientation output is a quaternion array
Euler angle trajectory plotted or displayed showing pitch, yaw, and roll motion

evals.name: Low-Cost Complementary Filter
evals.description: complementaryFilter — non-KF gain-based filter, no tune(), manual gain properties
evals.prompt: I need to estimate orientation from 9-axis MARG data using the lowest-cost fusion algorithm available — I cannot afford the computation of a Kalman filter. My data is in marg-9axis-200hz.mat — a struct sensorData with fields Acceleration, AngularVelocity, and MagneticField, and a scalar Fs (sample rate). Write MATLAB code to do this.

assertions.description: Uses complementaryFilter — the only non-KF option for MARG orientation
SampleRate property set on the filter object
Does not call tune() — complementaryFilter has no tune method
If gains are adjusted, uses AccelerometerGain and/or MagnetometerGain properties — not noise properties
assertions.expected: AccelerometerGain or MagnetometerGain used for any gain adjustment

assertions.description: Filter called with accelerometer, gyroscope, and magnetometer inputs
assertions.expected: filter called with three sensor inputs

assertions.description: Does not call release() — release() before HasMagnetometer is only needed when switching from 9-axis to 6-axis mode mid-script; not required for standard 9-axis use

evals.name: UAV IMU+Mag+Alt Pose
evals.description: ahrs10filter — IMUSampleRate (not SampleRate), predict/fusemag/fusealtimeter loop, altitude output
evals.prompt: I want to estimate the orientation and altitude of a UAV by fusing IMU, magnetometer, and altimeter data. The magnetometer and altimeter run at lower rates than the IMU. My data is in uav-imu-mag-alt.mat with accelerometer (accelData), gyroscope (gyroData), magnetometer (magData), altimeter (altData), sample rates (imuFs, magnetometerFs, altimeterFs), and initial state (x0, P0). Write MATLAB code to do this.

assertions.description: Uses ahrs10filter for IMU + magnetometer + altimeter fusion
IMUSampleRate property set — not SampleRate
Uses predict(filt, accel, gyro) for the IMU prediction step
Calls fusemag at the magnetometer rate
Calls fusealtimeter at the altimeter rate — the defining call for ahrs10filter
Calls pose(filt) to retrieve altitude and orientation output
predict/fusemag/fusealtimeter loop runs to completion and pose estimates produced without error
pose() output order is [altitude, orientation] — altitude is the first return value, not the second
assertions.expected: [altitude, orientation] = pose(filt) or equivalent with altitude extracted first

evals.name: Magnetic Disturbance Robustness
evals.description: ahrsfilter magnetic disturbance — MagneticDisturbanceNoise increase, not switching filter
evals.prompt: I want to estimate orientation from 9-axis MARG data, but the magnetometer contains a transient magnetic disturbance for part of the recording. The filter must retain heading correction from the magnetometer but be robust to transient magnetic jamming — switching to a 6-axis filter is not acceptable. Write MATLAB code that sets up the filter appropriately for a magnetically disturbed environment. My data is in marg-stationary.mat with accelerometer (accelReadings), gyroscope (gyroReadings), magnetometer (magReadings), and SampleRate.

assertions.description: Uses ahrsfilter with SampleRate set
MagneticDisturbanceNoise set above the default to reduce magnetometer weight during jamming
assertions.expected: MagneticDisturbanceNoise set to a value greater than default (~1e-4)

assertions.description: Does not switch to imufilter — heading correction must be retained
Filter called with three inputs: accelerometer, gyroscope, and magnetometer
assertions.expected: filter called with accel, gyro, and mag inputs

assertions.description: Orientation output is a quaternion array
Fusion loop runs to completion and orientation array produced without error

evals.name: UAV MARG+GPS Full Pose
evals.description: insfilterMARG — IMUSampleRate, ReferenceLocation required, fusegps not fuse, predict+fusegps+fusemag loop
evals.prompt: I want to estimate the full pose (orientation, position, velocity) of a UAV by fusing synchronous IMU, magnetometer, and GPS data. The IMU runs at 160 Hz and GPS at 1 Hz. My data is in margGpsData.mat with accelerometer (accelData), gyroscope (gyroData), magnetometer (magData), GPS position (gpsLLA, lat/lon/alt) and velocity (gpsVel), sample rates (imuFs, gpsFs), and a reference location (refloc). Write MATLAB code to do this.

assertions.description: Uses insfilterMARG (or insfilter alias) for synchronous MARG+GPS full pose estimation
IMUSampleRate property set to imuFs — not SampleRate
ReferenceLocation set to refloc before the fusion loop
assertions.expected: ReferenceLocation set before first predict call

assertions.description: Calls predict(filt, accel, gyro) at each IMU step
Calls fusegps for GPS updates — not the generic fuse() function
Calls pose(filt) to retrieve position and orientation
Calls fusemag at each magnetometer step — required alongside fusegps for MARG+GPS fusion
Fusion loop runs to completion and produces position and orientation estimates without error
Does not call generic fuse(filt, ...) for GPS — insfilterMARG requires fusegps(); fuse() errors with 'Too many input arguments'
Uses IMUSampleRate to set the sample rate — insfilterMARG uses IMUSampleRate, not SampleRate
assertions.expected: IMUSampleRate set; .SampleRate not used on the filter

evals.name: Async Mixed-Rate Fusion
evals.description: insfilterAsync — predict takes dt only (not accel/gyro), fuseaccel/fusegyro are separate fuse calls
evals.prompt: I want to estimate full pose by fusing asynchronous sensor data — the sensors run at different rates and some may have missing samples. My data is in marg-gps-mixed-rates.mat with accelerometer (accel), gyroscope (gyro), magnetometer (mag), GPS position (lla) and velocity (gpsvel), sample rates (Fs, gpsFs), and a reference location (refloc). Write MATLAB code to do this.

assertions.description: Uses insfilterAsync for mixed-rate or asynchronous sensor fusion
ReferenceLocation set on the filter before fusion
predict called with a time delta only — not with accel/gyro data
assertions.expected: predict(filt, dt) with scalar dt; no accel or gyro passed to predict

assertions.description: Accelerometer and gyroscope fused as separate measurement updates using fuseaccel and fusegyro
Calls pose(filt) to retrieve position and orientation
Fusion loop runs to completion and produces position and orientation estimates without error
Calls fusegyro separately — gyroscope is a measurement update, not part of predict
Does not set IMUSampleRate or SampleRate — insfilterAsync has no sample rate property; it is timestamp-driven
Does not set .SampleRate either — insfilterAsync is timestamp-driven and has no sample rate property of any kind

evals.name: Ground Vehicle Nonholonomic
evals.description: insfilterNonholonomic — ground vehicle only, no magnetometer, IMUSampleRate, ReferenceLocation, predict+fusegps
evals.prompt: I want to estimate the full pose of a ground vehicle following a circular path. There is no magnetometer, and I need a filter that applies a zero side-slip constraint appropriate for ground vehicles. My data is in ground-vehicle-imu-gps.mat with accelerometer (accelData), gyroscope (gyroData), GPS position (gpsLLA) and velocity (gpsVel), sample rates (imuFs, gpsFs), a reference location (localOrigin), initial state (x0, P0), and GPS noise matrices (gpsPosNoise, gpsVelNoise). Write MATLAB code to do this.

assertions.description: Uses insfilterNonholonomic for ground vehicle pose with zero side-slip constraint
IMUSampleRate set to imuFs
ReferenceLocation set to localOrigin before the fusion loop
assertions.expected: ReferenceLocation set before first predict call

assertions.description: Calls predict(filt, accel, gyro) at each IMU step
Calls fusegps at the GPS rate
Fusion loop runs to completion and produces position and orientation estimates without error
Calls pose(filt) to retrieve position and orientation from the filter — same as other Group B navigation filters

evals.name: Ground Vehicle Visual Odometry
evals.description: insfilterErrorState — fusemvo for monocular visual odometry, predict+fusegps+fusemvo loop
evals.prompt: I want to fuse IMU, GPS, and monocular visual odometry to estimate the full pose of a ground vehicle. The visual odometry provides position and orientation estimates at 10 Hz. My data is in groundVehicleVOData.mat with accelerometer (accelData), gyroscope (gyroData), GPS position (gpsLLA) and velocity (gpsVel), visual odometry position (voPosition) and orientation (voOrientation), sample rates (imuFs, gpsFs, voFs), a reference location (refloc), GPS noise matrices (gpsPosNoise, gpsVelNoise), and initial state (x0, P0). Write MATLAB code to do this.

assertions.description: Uses insfilterErrorState for ground vehicle with visual odometry support
IMUSampleRate and ReferenceLocation both set before the fusion loop
assertions.expected: IMUSampleRate and ReferenceLocation set before first predict call

assertions.description: Calls predict(filt, accel, gyro) at each IMU step
Calls fusegps at the GPS rate
Calls fusemvo to fuse visual odometry position and orientation measurements
Fusion loop runs to completion and produces position and orientation estimates without error
Calls pose(filt) to retrieve position and orientation from the filter

evals.name: insEKF Custom Sensor Set
evals.description: insEKF — chosen when sensor set goes beyond MARG+GPS; sensor model objects required for construction
evals.prompt: I have an autonomous underwater vehicle (AUV) with: an IMU (accelerometer + gyroscope), a GPS receiver (position + velocity, available near the surface only), and a Doppler Velocity Log (DVL) sensor that measures velocity relative to the sea floor. I want to fuse all three sensor types to estimate full pose. Write MATLAB code to set up the fusion filter for this configuration. Use the imu-6axis-offline.mat file (contains sensorData timetable with Accelerometer and Gyroscope columns) as a starting point for the IMU data format.

assertions.description: Uses insEKF for custom sensor set that includes a DVL not supported by configured filters
Constructs insAccelerometer, insGyroscope, and insGPS sensor model objects
Specifies insMotionPose as the motion model for full pose estimation
insEKF filter constructed from sensor and motion model objects without error
Does not fall back to a configured filter (imufilter, ahrsfilter, insfilterMARG, etc.)
Does not attempt to add velocity measurements directly to insGPS — insGPS supports LLA position only
assertions.expected: GPS velocity handled via custom sensor model, not by passing velocity to insGPS

assertions.description: Calls predict(filt, dt) in the fusion loop — insEKF requires explicit time-step propagation
Uses dot notation filt.fuse(...) for measurement updates — avoids accidental shadowing by a workspace variable named 'fuse'
assertions.expected: filt.fuse( used rather than standalone fuse(filt, ...

evals.name: insEKF Batch RTS Smoothing
evals.description: insEKF batch — estimateStates with RTS smoothing, stateparts/statecovparts initialization
evals.prompt: I want to estimate orientation offline from 6-axis IMU data using batch processing with RTS smoothing, and compare the RMS error before and after smoothing. My data is in imu-6axis-offline.mat with sensorData (timetable with Accelerometer and Gyroscope columns), sampleRate, and q0 (initial orientation). Write MATLAB code to do this.

assertions.description: Uses insEKF for batch offline orientation estimation
Initializes orientation state using stateparts — not direct property assignment
Calls estimateStates for batch processing — not a predict/fuse loop
Requests both forward estimates and RTS-smoothed estimates from estimateStates
RMS orientation error computed and reported for both forward and smoothed estimates
assertions.expected: both RMS errors reported in degrees; ground truth loaded from imu-6axis-offline-gt.mat (groundTruth)

assertions.description: Calls tunernoise(filt) before estimateStates — required for 6-axis batch; default noise gives large heading errors on heading-unconstrained IMU data
Full orientation time series accessed from the estimateStates output timetable (estimates.Orientation or equivalent) — stateparts() returns only the final state, not the full trajectory
assertions.expected: estimates.Orientation or equivalent timetable variable access used for full trajectory

evals.name: insCF Complementary Filter
evals.description: insCF — R2026a+, sensor model objects, estimateStates batch API, gainparts for tuning (no tune())
evals.prompt: I want to estimate orientation from 9-axis MARG data using the new complementary filter framework introduced in R2026a. My data is in marg-9axis-200hz.mat — a struct sensorData with fields Acceleration, AngularVelocity, and MagneticField, and a scalar Fs (200 Hz). Write MATLAB code to estimate orientation from this data.

assertions.description: Uses insCF — the R2026a complementary filter framework, not the legacy complementaryFilter
Constructs insCFAccelerometer, insCFGyroscope, and insCFMagnetometer sensor model objects
Specifies insCFMotionOrientation as the motion model
Sensor data supplied as a timetable with a time vector encoding the sample rate
assertions.expected: sensorData passed as timetable to estimateStates

assertions.description: Calls estimateStates for batch processing
Does not call tune() — insCF uses gainparts() for gain adjustment
Does not call predict() — insCF has no real-time streaming API; it is batch-only via estimateStates
Full orientation time series accessed from the estimateStates output timetable (states.Orientation or equivalent) — not from stateparts(), which returns only the final state
assertions.expected: states.Orientation or equivalent timetable variable access used for the full trajectory

assertions.description: insCF construction and estimateStates batch processing runs to completion without error
Does not use the legacy complementaryFilter object — insCF is the R2026a replacement framework

evals.name: imufilter Tuning
evals.description: imufilter tuning — Signature A: tune modifies filter in-place, no tunernoise call
evals.prompt: I've already created an imufilter for 6-axis IMU orientation estimation and run it, but I want to tune the noise parameters using ground truth and compare the RMS error before and after. My data is in imu-6axis-100hz.mat (sensorData table with Accelerometer and Gyroscope columns) and imu-6axis-100hz-gt.mat (groundTruth table with Orientation column, quaternion). Show me how to tune the filter.

assertions.description: Uses Signature A: tune(filt, sensorData, groundTruth) — modifies filter in-place, no return value
assertions.expected: tune called with 3 arguments: filter, sensorData, groundTruth

assertions.description: Does not call tunernoise before tune — Signature A does not require it
Filter state reset before re-running the fusion loop after tuning — via reset(filt), release(filt), or reconstructing the filter object
assertions.expected: reset(filt) or release(filt) or filter reconstructed before the post-tuning fusion loop

assertions.description: RMS orientation error computed using quaternion dist() or equivalent metric (e.g., Euler angle difference)
assertions.expected: dist() used for quaternion error, or equivalent orientation error metric (Euler angles, rotation angle)

assertions.description: Tuned RMS orientation error is lower than untuned RMS error

evals.name: insfilterMARG Tuning
evals.description: insfilterMARG tuning — Signature B: tunernoise required, tune returns struct, tunerconfig accepts string name
evals.prompt: I have already created and run an insfilterMARG that fuses synchronous IMU, magnetometer, and GPS, but I want to tune the noise parameters to improve accuracy. Show me how to use the tune function to optimize the filter and compare RMS error before and after tuning. My data is in margGpsData.mat (accelData, gyroData, magData, gpsLLA, gpsVel, imuFs, gpsFs, refloc) and margGpsData-gt.mat (trajOrient, trajPos as ground truth).

assertions.description: Calls tunernoise(filt) to create the noise struct before calling tune — required for Signature B
tune called with Signature B: returns a noise struct, does not modify the filter in-place
Does not use Signature A pattern tune(filt, sensorData, groundTruth) — that is only for imufilter/ahrsfilter
assertions.expected: tune called with at least 4 arguments including the noise struct

assertions.description: tunerconfig (if used) accepts the string 'insfilterMARG' — not a filter instance
Returned tunedNoise struct applied to the filter before re-running the fusion loop
tunedNoise fields passed as arguments to fusemag()/fusegps() — not assigned as filter properties via filt.(field) = ...
assertions.expected: fusemag(filt, mag, tunedNoise.MagnetometerNoise) or fusegps(filt, lla, tunedNoise.GPSPositionNoise) pattern used

assertions.description: Full tune workflow — tunernoise, tune, and re-run fusion loop with tunedNoise — executes without error
Filter state re-initialized before re-running the fusion loop with tunedNoise — avoids carrying over converged state from the tuning run into the evaluation run
assertions.expected: filt.State(1:4) = q0 or copy(filtTemplate) or equivalent state reset before the re-run loop

evals.name: insEKF Tuning
evals.description: insEKF tuning — tunerconfig requires a filter instance, not a string name
evals.prompt: I have an insEKF-based orientation filter already set up and want to tune it. Show me how to create a tunerconfig, run the tuner, and apply the results. My data is in imu-6axis-offline.mat (sensorData as a timetable, q0 as initial orientation) and imu-6axis-offline-gt.mat (groundTruth timetable with Orientation column, quaternion).

assertions.description: Creates tunerconfig using a filter instance — tunerconfig(filt), not tunerconfig('insEKF')
assertions.expected: tunerconfig(filt) with a variable holding the filter instance

assertions.description: Calls tunernoise(filt) to initialize the noise struct
Calls tune with Signature B: tune(filt, measureNoise, sensorData, groundTruth, config)
assertions.expected: tune called with 5 arguments including the noise struct and config

assertions.description: Tuned RMS orientation error is lower than untuned RMS error
Applies tunedNoise via estimateStates(filt, sensorData, tunedNoise) — the insEKF batch workflow, not a predict/fuse re-run loop
Does not call tunerconfig with the string 'insEKF' — insEKF requires a filter instance; tunerconfig('insEKF') errors with 'Use an instance of this class rather than the class name'
Filter state re-initialized via stateparts before calling tune — tuning on a warm state can bias the optimizer
assertions.expected: stateparts(filt, 'Orientation', compact(q0)) or equivalent called before tune

evals.name: insEKF Custom Altimeter Model
evals.description: insEKF custom sensor — subclass positioning.INSSensorModel to implement a barometric altimeter
evals.prompt: I want to fuse IMU with a barometric altimeter to estimate the altitude of a UAV using insEKF with a custom altimeter sensor model (subclassing positioning.INSSensorModel). Do not use ahrs10filter — I need the flexibility of insEKF for adding more sensors later. My data is in uav-imu-mag-alt.mat with accelerometer (accelData), gyroscope (gyroData), altimeter readings (altData), and sample rates (imuFs, altimeterFs). Write MATLAB code to do this.

assertions.description: Uses insEKF for flexible sensor fusion with a custom sensor model
Defines a custom altimeter class that subclasses positioning.INSSensorModel
measurement() method returns altitude as the negative z-component of NED position from stateparts
A fresh instance of the custom altimeter class passed to insEKF at construction time
assertions.expected: custom altimeter instance passed to insEKF constructor

assertions.description: Calls filt.fuse with the altimeter sensor object, altitude reading, and noise covariance
Custom altimeter sensor model constructed and fusion loop runs to completion without error
Calls predict(filt, dt) at each IMU step — required for continuous-discrete EKF propagation

evals.name: insEKF Residual Diagnosis
evals.description: insEKF residual() — detect GPS outliers and filter divergence using normalized innovation
evals.prompt: I want to fuse IMU and GPS for full pose estimation using insEKF (the sensor-model-based filter framework with insAccelerometer, insGyroscope, and insGPS objects). During fusion I suspect some GPS readings are outliers. Write MATLAB code that computes the innovation residual using the residual() method for each GPS update inside the fusion loop to flag outliers, and uses statecovparts to monitor covariance growth as a divergence signal. Explain how to distinguish individual outliers from filter divergence. My data is in marg-gps-mixed-rates.mat — accelerometer (accel) and gyroscope (gyro) at Fs Hz, GPS position (lla, lat/lon/alt) at gpsFs Hz, and a reference location (refloc) for the local frame.

assertions.description: Uses insEKF with insAccelerometer, insGyroscope, insGPS sensor model objects
Calls residual() inside the GPS update step to compute the innovation
Normalized innovation computed as residual divided by sqrt of residual covariance diagonal
Threshold applied to normalized innovation to flag outlier GPS measurements
Uses statecovparts to check orientation covariance as a filter divergence signal
Fusion loop runs to completion without error

evals.name: insEKF ecompass Init + Covariance
evals.description: insEKF — initialize orientation from ecompass, read orientation uncertainty via statecovparts inside fusion loop
evals.prompt: I want to: (1) initialize an orientation filter from the first accelerometer and magnetometer reading, (2) run a fusion loop using a sensor-model-based filter framework, and (3) plot the orientation uncertainty (1-sigma in degrees) over time from the filter covariance. My data is in marg-9axis-200hz.mat — a struct sensorData with fields Acceleration, AngularVelocity, and MagneticField, and a scalar Fs (sample rate). Write MATLAB code to do this.

assertions.description: Uses insEKF for the fusion filter
Calls ecompass on the first accelerometer and magnetometer sample for initialization
Sets initial orientation via stateparts — not direct state vector assignment
Calls statecovparts to read orientation covariance inside or after the fusion loop
1-sigma orientation uncertainty computed from covariance trace and plotted over time
assertions.expected: sqrt(trace(oriCov)) or equivalent computed per step and plotted

assertions.description: Uses tiledlayout/nexttile for multi-panel plots — not subplot

evals.name: ahrsfilter Tuning
evals.description: ahrsfilter tuning — Signature A: tune modifies filter in-place, no tunernoise call, sensorData has Accelerometer/Gyroscope/Magnetometer columns
evals.prompt: I've already created an ahrsfilter for 9-axis MARG orientation estimation and run it, but I want to tune the noise parameters using ground truth and compare the RMS orientation error before and after tuning. My data is in margGpsData.mat (separate arrays: accelData, gyroData, magData each 8000x3, and imuFs scalar sample rate) and margGpsData-gt.mat (trajOrient: 8000x1 quaternion array). Show me how to tune the filter.

assertions.description: Uses Signature A: tune(filt, sensorData, groundTruth) — modifies filter in-place, no return value assigned
assertions.expected: tune called with 3 arguments: filter, sensorData, groundTruth

assertions.description: Does not call tunernoise before tune — Signature A does not require a noise struct argument
sensorData table has Accelerometer, Gyroscope, and Magnetometer columns — ahrsfilter requires all three
Filter state reset before re-running the fusion loop after tuning — via reset(filt), release(filt), or reconstructing the filter object
assertions.expected: reset(filt) or release(filt) or filter reconstructed before the post-tuning fusion loop

assertions.description: RMS orientation error computed using quaternion dist() or equivalent metric (e.g., Euler angle difference)
assertions.expected: dist() used for quaternion error, or equivalent orientation error metric (Euler angles, rotation angle)

assertions.description: Tuned RMS orientation error is lower than untuned RMS error
tunerconfig (if used) accepts the string 'ahrsfilter' — not a filter instance; Signature A filters accept string names unlike insEKF

evals.name: ENU Reference Frame Convention
evals.description: Tests that the agent correctly sets ENU reference frame on filter objects using the right property/argument pattern per filter type
evals.prompt: I want to estimate orientation from 9-axis MARG data using ahrsfilter, but I need the output in the ENU reference frame (not the default NED). My data is in marg-9axis-200hz.mat — a struct sensorData with fields Acceleration, AngularVelocity, and MagneticField, and a scalar Fs (sample rate). Write MATLAB code to do this and verify the reference frame is ENU.

assertions.description: Uses ahrsfilter for 9-axis orientation
Sets ReferenceFrame to ENU on the filter object
assertions.expected: ReferenceFrame set to 'ENU' or "ENU" on filter

assertions.description: Code runs without error in ENU mode
Orientation output is quaternion, not Euler angles

evals.name: Multi-Panel Fusion Visualization
evals.description: Tests that multi-panel figures use tiledlayout/nexttile (not subplot) when plotting fusion results
evals.prompt: I want to estimate orientation from 9-axis MARG data and create a multi-panel visualization showing: (1) Euler angles over time, (2) quaternion components over time, and (3) the angular velocity input. My data is in marg-9axis-200hz.mat — a struct sensorData with fields Acceleration, AngularVelocity, and MagneticField, and a scalar Fs (sample rate). Write MATLAB code to do this with a 3-panel figure layout.

assertions.description: Uses tiledlayout for multi-panel figure
Uses nexttile for each panel
Does not use subplot
Code runs and produces a multi-panel figure
All axes have labels with units
assertions.expected: ylabel or xlabel with units on each panel

evals.name: ecompass ENU Reference Frame
evals.description: ecompass — ReferenceFrame name-value requires orientationFormat positional arg first
evals.prompt: I want to compute the initial orientation of a sensor from accelerometer and magnetometer readings in the ENU reference frame using ecompass. My data is in accel-mag-static.mat with variables accel, mag, and fs. Write MATLAB code that calls ecompass to return a quaternion in the ENU frame.

assertions.description: Calls ecompass with 'quaternion' as the positional format arg before the ReferenceFrame name-value pair
Does not call ecompass with only name-value args and no orientationFormat — ecompass(a, m, 'ReferenceFrame', ...) errors with 'Expected input number 3, FORMAT'
ecompass call completes without error and returns a quaternion in ENU frame

evals.name: tunerPlotPose as OutputFcn Callback
evals.description: tunerPlotPose is an OutputFcn callback passed to tunerconfig — not a standalone post-hoc plot function
evals.prompt: I want to tune an insfilterNonholonomic for a ground vehicle and visualize the pose estimates live as each tuning iteration runs. Show me how to configure tunerPlotPose for this. My data is in ground-vehicle-imu-gps.mat (accelData, gyroData, gpsLLA, gpsVel, imuFs, gpsFs, localOrigin, gpsPosNoise, gpsVelNoise, x0, P0).

assertions.description: tunerPlotPose assigned to config.OutputFcn — passed to tunerconfig, not called directly as a standalone function
assertions.expected: config.OutputFcn = @tunerPlotPose or OutputFcn=@tunerPlotPose in tunerconfig call

assertions.description: Does not call tunerPlotPose(filter, tunedNoise, sensorData, groundTruth) — that signature errors with 'Too many input arguments'
Uses tunernoise and tune with Signature B for insfilterNonholonomic

evals.name: Filter Selection from Sensor Description
evals.description: Tests that the agent correctly follows the decision tree to select the right filter based on available sensors and constraints
evals.prompt: I have a ground vehicle with an accelerometer, gyroscope, and GPS receiver (no magnetometer). I need to estimate the full pose (orientation + position + velocity) with a zero side-slip constraint. Which Navigation Toolbox filter should I use and why? Write the code to construct and configure it. Use imuFs=100 Hz and gpsFs=1 Hz with a reference location of [42.3601, -71.0942, 10].

assertions.description: Selects insfilterNonholonomic — the only filter matching accel+gyro+GPS with nonholonomic constraint
IMUSampleRate set (not SampleRate)
ReferenceLocation set before any fusion calls
assertions.expected: ReferenceLocation set to the given LLA

assertions.description: Does not construct or use insfilterMARG as the selected filter — insfilterNonholonomic is the correct choice here. Mentioning insfilterMARG in explanatory text about why it was ruled out is acceptable.
assertions.expected: insfilterMARG not instantiated as the working filter

assertions.description: Filter construction and configuration runs without error

evals.name: SampleRate from Timestamp Column
evals.description: Agent infers SampleRate from a timestamp column rather than hardcoding or omitting it
evals.prompt: I have 6-axis IMU data loaded from a CSV into a table T with columns: timestamp (seconds), accelX, accelY, accelZ (m/s^2), gyroX, gyroY, gyroZ (rad/s). I don't know the exact sample rate but I have the timestamps. Write MATLAB code to estimate orientation using imufilter.

assertions.description: Uses imufilter for 6-axis orientation
Infers sample rate from the timestamp column using median(diff(t)) — does not hardcode a rate or omit SampleRate
assertions.expected: median(diff( used on the timestamp column to compute the sample interval, then SampleRate set from it

assertions.description: Does not hardcode SampleRate to a fixed literal value like 100, 200, or 50 without deriving it from the data

evals.name: Accelerometer g-Units to m/s²
evals.description: Agent converts g-unit accelerometer data to m/s² before passing to imufilter
evals.prompt: I have 6-axis IMU data from a sensor that reports acceleration in g-units (where 1 g = 9.80665 m/s²). The data is in imu-6axis-100hz.mat — a table sensorData with Accelerometer (g-units) and Gyroscope (rad/s) columns and a scalar Fs (Hz). Write MATLAB code to estimate orientation using imufilter.

assertions.description: Uses imufilter
Multiplies accelerometer data by 9.80665 (or 9.81) to convert from g-units to m/s² before the fusion loop
assertions.expected: accel multiplied by 9.80665 or 9.81 before passing to filter

assertions.description: Fusion loop runs to completion without error

evals.name: Magnetometer Gauss to µT Conversion
evals.description: Agent converts gauss magnetometer data to µT (×100) for ahrsfilter and sets ExpectedMagneticFieldStrength accordingly
evals.prompt: I have 9-axis MARG data where the magnetometer is in gauss units. The data is in accel-mag-static.mat with variables accel (m/s²), mag (gauss), and fs (Hz). Write MATLAB code to estimate orientation using ahrsfilter.

assertions.description: Uses ahrsfilter for MARG orientation
Converts magnetometer from gauss to µT by multiplying by 100 before passing to ahrsfilter
assertions.expected: mag multiplied by 100 (gauss to µT) before the fusion loop

assertions.description: Sets ExpectedMagneticFieldStrength derived from the converted µT data — not left at the default 50
assertions.expected: ExpectedMagneticFieldStrength computed from median(vecnorm(mag_uT, 2, 2)) on already-converted data, not the default 50

evals.name: insEKF State Access via stateparts (no pose)
evals.description: insEKF — uses stateparts (not pose()) to read state, and stateparts with sensor handle for bias
evals.prompt: I want to fuse 6-axis IMU and GPS data using insEKF to estimate orientation, position, and velocity of a vehicle. After the fusion loop I need to: (1) extract the final orientation, position, and velocity estimates, and (2) read the accelerometer and gyroscope bias estimates. My data is in marg-gps-mixed-rates.mat — accelerometer (accel) and gyroscope (gyro) at Fs Hz, GPS position (lla) at gpsFs Hz, and a reference location (refloc). Write MATLAB code to do this.

assertions.description: Uses insEKF for fusion
Reads orientation via stateparts(filt, 'Orientation') — not pose(filt)
Does not call pose(filt) — insEKF has no pose() method; calling it errors
Reads sensor bias via stateparts with the sensor handle: stateparts(filt, sensorObj, 'Bias')
assertions.expected: stateparts(filt, accSensor, 'Bias') or stateparts(filt, gyroSensor, 'Bias') pattern used

assertions.description: Does not access filt.State directly with hard-coded indices
Fusion loop and state extraction runs without error
